subject: Geology
title: Island Arcs
description: Learn Island Arcs in one minute.

hook.text: Japan, Indonesia, and the Philippines are all volcanic island arcs, born where one ocean plate dives under another.

buildup.text: Island arcs form at ocean-ocean convergent boundaries where the subducting plate releases water into the mantle.

discovery.text: That water lowers the mantle's melting point, creating magma that rises to build a curved chain of volcanoes.

twist.text: The arc shape comes from the geometry of subduction on a sphere. A flat plane cutting a sphere creates a curve.

climax.text: Island arcs are some of the most geologically active places on Earth: earthquakes, volcanoes, and tsunamis.

punchline.text: Sinking ocean plates build volcanic islands.

quiz.question: How do island arcs form?
options: From oceanic-oceanic subduction creating volcanic chains
